paragraphs.en: One of my bad habits is saying "busy" when people ask me how I'm doing. Sometimes it's because I actually am busy, but other times it's because that's what I think I'm supposed to say. That's what important people say. That's what people who get promoted say.
paragraphs.zh: 我有个坏习惯：别人问我过得怎么样，我总说「忙」。有时确实很忙，但有时只是觉得应该这么说。重要人物都这么说，升职的人也这么说。

paragraphs.en: But working long hours doesn't drive better results. Never taking a vacation won't lead to a promotion. So why are we so proud to talk about how busy we are all the time? In 2016, researchers from Columbia, Harvard, and Georgetown conducted a study to figure it out. They found busy people are perceived to be of high status, and interestingly, these status attributions are heavily influenced by our own beliefs about social mobility.
paragraphs.zh: 但长时间工作并不能带来更好的结果，从不休假也不会带来晋升。那为什么我们总是很得意地谈论自己有多忙？2016年，哥伦比亚、哈佛和乔治城的研究人员做了一项研究来探究这一现象。他们发现忙碌的人被视为地位较高，而有趣的是，这种地位认知很大程度上受我们对社会流动性信念的影响。

paragraphs.en: In other words, the more we believe that one has the opportunity for success based on hard work, the more we tend to think that people who skip leisure and work all the time are of higher standing. That's why we feel like we have to appear busy, and there's a real perception that if someone is knee-deep in meetings, emails and stress, then they're probably a big deal. This culture of busyness is making it hard for employees to find work-life balance. According to a recent study, one in five highly engaged employees are at risk of burnout.
paragraphs.zh: 换句话说，我们越相信努力工作能带来成功，就越觉得那些从不休息的人地位更高。所以我们觉得自己必须显得很忙，而且的确有一种认知：如果一个人淹没在会议、邮件和压力中，那他可能很重要。这种忙碌文化让员工难以找到工作与生活的平衡。研究显示，五分之一高度投入的员工面临精疲力竭的风险。

paragraphs.en: Personally, I'm going to stop saying "busy" when people ask me how I am. It sounds self-righteous and sets the wrong tone. Phrases like "I have limited access to email" and "I'll respond as soon as I get back" sound like you're being held against your will from working as opposed to making the most of your time off.
paragraphs.zh: 就我个人而言，我不会再说「忙」了——这听起来很自以为是，而且定下了错误的基调。「我对邮件的访问权限有限」「我一回来就回复」这类话听起来像是被迫无法工作，而不是在享受休息时光。

paragraphs.en: That's why we recently launched the Out of Office Email Generator, a free tool you can use before your next long weekend or trip. You can share loud and proud that you won't be checking email until you're back. Managers need to think twice about emailing their teams on the weekend and talking about how busy they are. Leaders should take time off themselves and encourage employees to do the same.
paragraphs.zh: 所以我们最近推出了「外出自动回复生成器」，一个免费工具，可以在下次长周末或旅行前使用。你可以大大方方地告诉大家，回来之前不会查看邮件。管理者要三思而后行，别在周末给团队发邮件、谈论自己有多忙。领导者应该自己休假，并鼓励员工也这样做。
